data.description:
## Context
A plan-review attestation binds to the latest MATERIAL ticket-event commit, so editing the plan invalidates it.
But the underlying CODE the plan reasons about can drift AFTER the review without any ticket edit, silently
staling the attestation. (Spun out of `5fd2`; was follow-on F9.)

## Success Criteria
- An attestation is invalidated (or flagged stale) when the code it was grounded against drifts materially,
  not only when the ticket is edited.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A mechanism detects material drift in the code the review was grounded against (e.g. the resolved edit-set / cited files).
- [ ] Drift invalidates or flags the attestation, prompting re-review; cheap enough not to tax hot paths.
- [ ] Tested: code drift after a signed review -> attestation no longer valid.
data.title: Code-drift invalidation for plan-review attestations
